page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e4.small is an entry-level virtual machine instance based on the KVM hypervisor. It features a single shared vCPU powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8:1. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this instance in the lower tiers of performance. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance score is 1086.18, which is in the bottom 25%, while the multi-core score is identical due to the single-core limitation, placing it in the bottom 10%. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with read-only small block cached memory bandwidth at 75.4 GB/sec and uncached large block read bandwidth at 8.16 GB/sec. Geekbench scores reflect this positioning, with a single-core score of 871.0 and a multi-core score of 877.0.

The shared CPU allocation and lack of local storage make this instance highly cost-efficient for lightweight, non-intensive workloads where resource density is prioritized over raw compute power. It is suitable for basic web serving, small-scale Redis caching, lightweight background tasks, and development or testing environments that do not require sustained high CPU performance.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e4.small is an entry-level, KVM-based virtual machine featuring 1 shared vCPU from an Intel Xeon 8163 processor clocked at 2.5 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPU resources, and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Performance benchmarks place this instance in the lower percentiles, with a Geekbench single-core score of 871.0 and a stress-ng CPU score of 1086.18. Given its shared CPU architecture and high memory-to-core ratio, it offers a cost-effective solution for lightweight workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, development environments, and low-throughput background tasks.
